retry_reason: put_device is unconditional and cannot fail; retrying would cause a double put, leading to use-after-free or premature deallocation.
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper that decrements the reference count of the device (ctrl->dev) associated with the nvme controller. It is called during controller cleanup to release a device reference that was acquired earlier (typically via get_device during controller initialization). After this call, the caller must not access the device through the released reference. The function is unconditional, returns no status, and is the natural inverse of a get_device operation.

judge.reason: The function's sole purpose is to call put_device on ctrl->dev, decrementing the device's reference count. There is no additional business logic; it is a pure put wrapper.

check.reason: put_device(ctrl->dev) releases a reference on the device object. The access path from parameter 1 (ctrl) via dev.kobj.kref.refcount correctly traces to the refcount field. The type chain from nvme_ctrl to device to kobject to kref to refcount_t is consistent with the callee info and kernel structures.
